2604.11575.title: MIXAR: Scaling Autoregressive Pixel-based Language Models to Multiple Languages and Scripts
2604.11575.paper_summary: Pixel-based language models are gaining momentum as alternatives to traditional token-based approaches, promising to circumvent tokenization challenges. However, the inherent perceptual diversity across languages poses a significant hurdle for multilingual generalization in pixel space. This paper introduces MIXAR, the first generative pixel-based language model trained on eight different languages utilizing a range of different scripts. We empirically evaluate MIXAR against previous pixel-based models as well as comparable tokenizer-based models, demonstrating substantial performance improvement on discriminative and generative multilingual tasks. Additionally, we show how MIXAR is robust to languages never seen during the training. These results are further strengthened when scaling the model to 0.5B parameters which not only improves its capabilities in generative tasks like LAMBADA but also its robustness when challenged with input perturbations such as orthographic attacks.
2604.11575.paper_summary_zh: 像素基礎的語言模型作為傳統基於標記的方法的替代方案正在獲得動力，承諾能夠繞過標記化的挑戰。<lb/>然而，語言之間固有的感知多樣性對於像素空間中的多語言泛化構成了重大障礙。<lb/>本文介紹了MIXAR，第一個在八種不同語言上訓練的生成型像素基礎語言模型，利用了一系列不同的字母系統。<lb/>我們對MIXAR進行了實證評估，與之前的像素基礎模型以及可比較的基於標記的模型進行比較，顯示出在判別性和生成性多語言任務上有顯著的性能提升。<lb/>此外，我們展示了MIXAR對於在訓練期間從未見過的語言的穩健性。<lb/>當將模型擴展到0.5B參數時，這些結果進一步加強，不僅改善了其在生成任務（如LAMBADA）中的能力，還提高了在面對輸入擾動（如正字法攻擊）時的穩健性。
2604.11575.author: Chen Hu et.al.
2604.11575.authors: Chen Hu, Yintao Tai, Antonio Vergari, Frank Keller, Alessandro Suglia
